seo.title: Smolagents Guardrails for E-Commerce
seo.description: Compliance guide for Smolagents agents in e-commerce. Meet FTC advertising guidelines, CCPA/GDPR requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: E-Commerce teams using Smolagents face heightened risks from AI-generated code that could produce unsubstantiated product claims, inaccurate pricing, or non-compliant payment handling, violating FTC guidelines and data privacy laws like CCPA/GDPR. Without guardrails, multi-step reasoning errors in lightweight agents can compound into refund disputes, PCI DSS breaches, and ADA non-compliance, leading to customer churn and enforcement actions. Vex provides essential verification to ensure Smolagents outputs align with industry regulations while maintaining simplicity.

complianceRequirements.description: Product claims must be substantiated with evidence; AI-generated descriptions cannot exaggerate benefits or mislead consumers.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex verifies agent outputs against factual product data, blocking unsubstantiated claims before customer exposure.

complianceRequirements.description: Customer data handling must include consent mechanisms and data minimization; agents cannot log or process PII without verification.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex scans for PII exposure in code and enforces privacy rules, preventing non-compliant data flows.

complianceRequirements.description: Payment processing code must not hardcode credentials or transmit card data insecurely.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex audits generated payment code for tokenization and secure API usage, ensuring PCI compliance.

complianceRequirements.description: Dynamic content like product pages must support screen readers and keyboard navigation.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex checks agent-generated HTML/JS for WCAG 2.1 AA standards, flagging accessibility violations.

industryRisks.name: Inaccurate Pricing Generation
industryRisks.description: Multi-step reasoning errors lead to wrong discounts or tax calculations, causing refund disputes.
industryRisks.example: Agent miscalculates 20% off $99 item as $59 instead of $79.20, leading to undercharges.

industryRisks.name: Unsubstantiated Product Claims
industryRisks.description: Code agents fabricate health benefits without evidence, violating FTC rules.
industryRisks.example: Vitamin listing claims 'cures fatigue' based on hallucinated studies.

industryRisks.name: PII Leakage in Tool Calls
industryRisks.description: Tool agents misinterpret descriptions and log customer emails unencrypted.
industryRisks.example: Search tool stores query 'John Doe email' in plaintext logs.

industryRisks.name: Missing Refund Policy Disclosure
industryRisks.description: Generated pages omit return policy links, misleading buyers.
industryRisks.example: Product page lacks '30-day returns' notice required by state law.

implementation.title: Verify Pricing Calculations
implementation.description: Wrap smolagents pricing agent with Vex to catch arithmetic errors in dynamic pricing.

implementation.title: Secure Payment Tool Calls
implementation.description: Ensure payment processing code uses tokenization and avoids PII.

implementation.title: Check Product Claims Substantiation
implementation.description: Validate generated descriptions against verified product specs.

caseStudyScenario.heading: Smolagents in E-Commerce: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario: Fashion retailer TrendThread deployed Smolagents to power dynamic pricing and personalized product recommendations across their Python-based storefront. The lightweight framework enabled rapid iteration on discount logic and description generation, but without oversight, the pricing agent began compounding errors: a 25% sitewide sale miscalculated taxes in multi-step reasoning, undercharging customers by 8-12%. Simultaneously, the description agent hallucinated 'organic cotton' for synthetic blends, violating FTC guidelines, while tool calls for abandoned cart recovery leaked emails due to misread tool schemas. Within weeks, refund requests spiked 40%, and a viral social complaint threatened brand trust.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: Integrating Vex.verify() around agent.run() calls transformed compliance. Pricing verification caught 97% of calculation errors pre-checkout, saving $12K in refunds monthly. FTC claim checks blocked unsubstantiated descriptors, and PCI scans ensured payment tools tokenized correctly. Post-Vex, customer satisfaction rose 22%, disputes dropped to near-zero, and TrendThread scaled Smolagents confidently across Black Friday without regulatory incidents.

cta.heading: Comply with E-Commerce Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Install Vex SDK today and wrap your Smolagents runs to eliminate compliance risks—pip install vex-sdk.
